title: _interpreters.exec under OOM frees an invalid cross-interpreter excinfo — _excinfo_clear_type segfault (Python/crossinterp.c:1319)
description: _interpreters.exec captures a subinterpreter exception into a _PyXI_excinfo; under OOM the capture fails partway and the cleanup path _PyXI_FreeExcInfo -> _PyXI_excinfo_clear -> _excinfo_clear_type runs on an uninitialized/dangling info, dereferencing info->builtin at crossinterp.c:1319 -> SIGSEGV on all builds.
notes: Wild-pointer deref: _excinfo_clear_type reads info->builtin (crossinterp.c:1319) on a _PyXI_excinfo that an OOM during _interpreters.exec's exception capture left invalid (or unzeroed garbage), yet _PyXI_FreeExcInfo was still called on it. Reproduces on ALL four builds (not assert-gated). Distinct from OOM-0014/0016 (_interpchannels/_interpqueues) and OOM-0026 (handle_channel_error) -- this is crossinterp.c excinfo cleanup. Same free/clear-partially-initialized-struct-under-OOM theme as OOM-0024/0030. Fix: zero-init the _PyXI_excinfo + don't FreeExcInfo a not-fully-initialized struct on the exec failure path. Minimization PARTIAL/vehicle-confirmed (vehicle_source.py segvs deterministically; the _interpreters.create+exec reduction didn't hit the window). Found by the LOCAL systemd fleet against the current 29-bug catalog.
